samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal. Used to trigger write operations.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal. Used to trigger read operations.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data to be written into the FIFO.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data read from the FIFO.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="8">FIFO bit width.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="16">FIFO depth.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Dual-port RAM instantiation
        <instance id="ram" module_id="dual_port_RAM" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]" depth_description="[DEPTH-1:0]">
            <port id="wclk" connection="wclk"/>
            <port id="wenc" connection="wen"/>
            <port id="waddr" connection="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="wdata" connection="wdata"/>
            <port id="rclk" connection="rclk"/>
            <port id="renc" connection="ren"/>
            <port id="raddr" connection="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="rdata" connection="rdata"/>
        </instance>
        
        // Write pointer management
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))-1:0]">Binary representation of the write address.</reg>
        <always sensitive="posedge wclk or negedge wrstn">
            if (!wrstn) begin
                waddr_bin <= 0;
            end else if (winc && !wfull) begin
                waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
            end
        </always>
        
        // Read pointer management
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))-1:0]">Binary representation of the read address.</reg>
        <always sensitive="posedge rclk or negedge rrstn">
            if (!rrstn) begin
                raddr_bin <= 0;
            end else if (rinc && !rempty) begin
                raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
            end
        </always>
        
        // Gray code conversion for write pointer
        <wire id="wptr" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))]:0]">Gray code representation of the write pointer.</wire>
        <assign wptr[0] = waddr_bin[0];>
        <assign wptr[1] = waddr_bin[1] ^ waddr_bin[0];>
        <assign wptr[2] = waddr_bin[2] ^ waddr_bin[1];>
        <assign wptr[3] = waddr_bin[3] ^ waddr_bin[2];>
        
        // Gray code conversion for read pointer
        <wire id="rptr" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))]:0]">Gray code representation of the read pointer.</wire>
        <assign rptr[0] = raddr_bin[0];>
        <assign rptr[1] = raddr_bin[1] ^ raddr_bin[0];>
        <assign rptr[2] = raddr_bin[2] ^ raddr_bin[1];>
        <assign rptr[3] = raddr_bin[3] ^ raddr_bin[2];>
        
        // Buffer registers for synchronized pointers
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))]:0]">Buffer register for write pointer synchronization.</reg>
        <always sensitive="posedge wclk or negedge wrstn">
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        </always>
        
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))]:0]">Buffer register for read pointer synchronization.</reg>
        <always sensitive="posedge rclk or negedge rrstn">
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_buff <= rptr;
            end
        </always>
        
        // Full/empty signal generation
        <reg id="wfull" width="1">Write full flag.</reg>
        <always sensitive="wptr_buff or rptr_buff">
            wfull <= (wptr_buff == {~rptr_buff[3], rptr_buff[2:0]});
        </always>
        
        <reg id="rempty" width="1">Read empty flag.</reg>
        <always sensitive="wptr_buff or rptr_buff">
            rempty <= (wptr_buff == rptr_buff);
        </always>
        
        // Control signals for RAM
        <wire id="wen" width="1">Write enable signal for RAM.</wire>
        <assign wen = winc && !wfull;>
        
        <wire id="ren" width="1">Read enable signal for RAM.</wire>
        <assign ren = rinc && !rempty;>
        
        // Connect outputs
        <assign rdata = ram.rdata;>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Asynchronously manages read and write operations using dual-port RAM and pointer buffers to handle full/empty conditions.</implementation>
</module>
